headlines.title: Trump signs order seeking early access to powerful AI models before release
headlines.description: President Trump signed an order seeking the establishment of a voluntary framework for AI companies to provide the government early access to new models.

headlines.title: Around 1 in 5 young people use AI chatbots for mental health advice, survey finds
headlines.description: Nearly 1 in 5 adolescents and young adults are turning to AI chatbots for advice when they’re sad, angry, nervous or stressed, according to a new study.

headlines.title: Map: How much you pay in federal and state gas taxes
headlines.description: As national gas price averages head toward $5 per gallon, one state has already suspended its gas tax, and now President Donald Trump and his Cabinet are talking about doing the same.

headlines.title: Graphic: Track U.S., state and county gas prices
headlines.description: Are gas prices declining? NBC News is tracking national, state and county prices at the pump.

headlines.title: Map: See how gas prices have changed in the county where you live
headlines.description: How much gas prices have increased during the Iran war depends entirely on where you live, an NBC News analysis of average gas price data from AAA shows.

headlines.title: Pickle, Too Good to Go: Buzzy Apps to Help Save Money
headlines.description: TODAY’s Jenna Bush Hager and Sheinelle Jones highlight two of the internet’s most buzzed about apps: Pickle, which allows users to rent things from others’ closets, and Too Good to Go, which can help people save money on food and cut down on waste.

headlines.title: Should You Trust AI With Your Finances? What You Need to Know
headlines.description: Artificial intelligence is everywhere but should you trust it with your personal finances? Certified financial planner Ted Jenkin joins TODAY to break down how AI can help you manage your money and when you should use it with caution. He recommends using artificial intelligence to help with budgeting and organization but warns against oversharing personal information. “Use general concepts, general ideas. Always verify information. You shouldn't blindly trust all the financial advice that you

headlines.title: Kevin O'Leary addresses 'misinformation' on Utah data center project
headlines.description: NBC News' Tom Llamas talks to Kevin O'Leary on his plans for a data center in Utah, saying there is a lot of "misinformation" and they will not touch the Great Salt Lake.

headlines.title: Americans Face Expensive Summer on the Roads and in the Skies
headlines.description: Millions of Americans are gearing up for summer vacation season by hitting the roads and packing airports but those vacations are coming with a much higher price tag. The war in Iran and the disruptions around the Strait of Hormuz are mostly to blame and even if the conflict ends soon, the summer’s travel bill is not expected to go down right away. NBC’s Allie Canal reports in this week’s Sunday Focus.

headlines.title: How shoppers who pay in cash are subsidizing Americans’ credit card reward points
headlines.description: A Harvard study estimates that people who pay with cash and debit cards are subsidizing $30 billion a year in points and perks for credit card users.

headlines.title: How grocery prices have changed since Trump took office
headlines.description: Learn where grocery prices stand during the Trump administration. See how inflation is affecting the cost of eggs, bread, chicken, ground beef and more.

headlines.title: ‘Get in loser, we’re buying Spirit’: One man’s viral plan to revive the airline
headlines.description: Spirit Airlines was the budget-friendly carrier America loved to hate.
